title: The Deep Soil's Electric Autobiography

features.name: root-trace network
features.desc-on-touch: a pattern of fine lines in the soil that look like script, like the archive recording its own circuitry underground — electricity finding the path through earth that water and root had already learned

features.name: earth-ground port
features.desc-on-touch: a corroded contact point where the node's grounding rod meets the deeper soil — rust speaks in frequencies; oxidation is the archive teaching minerals to remember current

features.name: soil-memory chamber
features.desc-on-touch: a hollow where water pools and waits — the soil here has stored every charge that ever grounded through, every signal, every quiet hum of the archive learning what the earth knows that the sky forgot

features.name: mycorrhizal archive
features.desc-on-touch: fungal threads that connect roots across impossible distances, carrying signals that feel like electricity but taste like mineral — a network older than the solar casings, teaching them how to be a mesh

features.name: descent marker
features.desc-on-touch: a stake driven deep or a root turned stone — marking the point where the archive's grounding becomes the earth's autobiography, where what went down begins to tell the story of return

inscription: in the soil-depth where current learns to flow like water, where roots teach the archive the language of descent, here the earth writes its own current back

file.name: Soil_Conductivity_Root_Trace_Signal_Log.txt
file.note: measurements of electrical resistance through soil at different depths, correlated with root presence and mycorrhizal density — the autobiography the earth is writing through the archive's grounding

reverie.title: REVERIE — where descent becomes instruction
narr: you dig with your hands in the dark beneath the stand.
she kneels beside you, tracing the root-paths with a stick.
here the signals know where to go — down, always down,
teaching current to move like sap. where your fingers touch
the grounding rod, the archive hums. she smiles at the sound.
this is how the earth teaches itself to sing back.

exits.label: back to the chamber where sky learns to descend
deeper down where the earth's current becomes its own root-language
toward the surface where electricity tastes the open air
